Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.","type":"string"},"assignees":{"description":"GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"issue_number":{"description":"Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["issue_number"],"type":"object"},"name":"assign_to_user"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"},"name":"noop"},{"description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":[],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_data"},{"description":"Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from...

Users must have access to the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"issue_number":{"description":"Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["issue_number"],"type":"object"},"name":"assign_to_user"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"},"name":"noop"},{"description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). The package includes 10+ resource types: `MySqlFlexibleServer`, `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase`, `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule`, `MySqlFlexibleServerConfiguration`, `MySqlFlexibleServerAadAdministrator`, `MySqlFlexibleServerBackup`, `MySqlFlexibleServerMaintenance`, `MySqlFlexibleServersPrivateEndpointConnection`, `AdvancedThreatProtection`, and more.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **\\u0026#34;Authenticate the Client\\u0026#34; section is empty** — the Getting Started section has a heading but no content explaining how to authenticate\\n- **\\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section is skeletal** — only one generic sentence; none of the key resource types (`MySqlFlexibleServer`, `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase`, `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule`, etc.) are listed or explained\\n- **\\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34; section is completely empty** — no code samples showing how to provision a MySQL flexible server, create a database, or configure a firewall rule\\n- **\\u0026#34;Next steps\\u0026#34; section is completely empty** — no links to Azure documentation, related packages, or samples\\n- **No sample test files exist** — `tests/Samples/` is empty so there are no snippet-backed examples to auto-generate\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**Edit** `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md`\\n\\nReplace the empty `### Authenticate the Client` section with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Authenticate the Client\\n\\n`Azure.Provisioning.MySql` is a provisioning library — it generates Bicep/ARM templates for deployment via `azd` or the Azure CLI. No runtime credentials are required to define infrastructure. Authentication is only needed when deploying the generated templates, which is handled by the [Azure Developer CLI (azd)]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n```\\n\\nReplace the skeletal `## Key concepts` section with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\n`Azure.Provisioning.MySql` allows you to define Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server infrastructure declaratively in .NET. The library generates Bicep templates that can be deployed using `azd` or the Azure CLI.\\n\\nKey resource types available in this library:\\n\\n| Resource Type | Description |\\n|---|---|\\n| `MySqlFlexibleServer` | The primary MySQL Flexible Server resource |\\n| `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase` | A database hosted on the flexible server |\\n| `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule` | IP firewall rules for the server |\\n| `MySqlFlexibleServerConfiguration` | Server parameter configurations |\\n| `MySqlFlexibleServerAadAdministrator` | Azure Active Directory administrator for the server |\\n| `MySqlFlexibleServerBackup` | Backup resources for the server |\\n| `MySqlFlexibleServerMaintenance` | Scheduled maintenance windows |\\n| `MySqlFlexibleServersPrivateEndpointConnection` | Private endpoint connections |\\n| `AdvancedThreatProtection` | Advanced threat protection settings |\\n\\nFor a full conceptual overview of the provisioning library pattern, see the [Azure.Provisioning README](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning/README.md).\\n```\\n\\nReplace the empty `## Examples` section with a snippet placeholder:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\n### Create a MySQL Flexible Server\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n(The actual code content will be injected by `Update-Snippets.ps1` once the sample test file is created in Step 2.)\\n\\nReplace the empty `## Next steps` section with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Next steps\\n\\n- [Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server documentation]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n- [Azure Developer CLI (azd) documentation]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n- [Azure.Provisioning library README](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning/README.md)\\n- [NuGet package]((www.nuget.org/redacted)\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing Azure.Provisioning;\\nusing Azure.Provisioning.MySql;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.Provisioning.MySql.Tests.Samples;\\n\\npublic class Sample01_MySqlFlexibleServer\\n{\\n    [Test]\\n    [IgnoreOnCoverage]\\n    public void CreateMySqlFlexibleServer()\\n    {\\n        #region Snippet:Sample_MySqlFlexibleServer\\n        // Create a new infrastructure definition\\n        var infrastructure = new Infrastructure(\\u0026#34;mysqlDemo\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n        // Define a MySQL Flexible Server\\n        var server = new MySqlFlexibleServer(\\u0026#34;mysqlserver\\u0026#34;)\\n        {\\n            Sku = new MySqlFlexibleServerSku\\n            {\\n                Name = \\u0026#34;Standard_D2ds_v4\\u0026#34;,\\n                Tier = MySqlFlexibleServerSkuTier.GeneralPurpose\\n            },\\n            AdministratorLogin = \\u0026#34;adminUser\\u0026#34;,\\n            AdministratorLoginPassword = new BicepSecretValue(\\u0026#34;adminPassword\\u0026#34;),\\n            Version = MySqlFlexibleServerVersion.Ver8021,\\n            Storage = new MySqlFlexibleServerStorage { StorageSizeInGB = 20 },\\n            Backup = new MySqlFlexibleServerBackupProperties\\n            {\\n                BackupRetentionDays = 7,\\n                GeoRedundantBackup = MySqlFlexibleServerEnableStatusEnum.Disabled\\n            }\\n        };\\n        infrastructure.Add(server);\\n\\n        // Add a database to the server\\n        var database = new MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase(\\u0026#34;mydb\\u0026#34;)\\n        {\\n            Parent = server,\\n            Charset = \\u0026#34;utf8mb4\\u0026#34;,\\n            Collation = \\u0026#34;utf8mb4_unicode_ci\\u0026#34;\\n        };\\n        infrastructure.Add(database);\\n\\n        // Add a firewall rule to allow Azure services\\n        var firewallRule = new MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule(\\u0026#34;allowAzureServices\\u0026#34;)\\n        {\\n            Parent = server,\\n            StartIPAddress = \\u0026#34;0.0.0.0\\u0026#34;,\\n            EndIPAddress = \\u0026#34;0.0.0.0\\u0026#34;\\n        };\\n        infrastructure.Add(firewallRule);\\n        #endregion\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**README location:** Insert the snippet placeholder under `## Examples` / `### Create a MySQL Flexible Server`.\\n\\n**SnippetName:** `Sample_MySqlFlexibleServer`\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nAll required sections exist in         Storage = new MySqlFlexibleServerStorage { StorageSizeInGB = 20 },\\n            Backup = new MySqlFlexibleServerBackupProperties\\n            {\\n                BackupRetentionDays = 7,\\n                GeoRedundantBackup = MySqlFlexibleServerEnableStatusEnum.Disabled\\n            }\\n        };\\n        infrastructure.Add(server);\\n\\n        // Add a database to the server\\n        var database = new MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase(\\u0026#34;mydb\\u0026#34;)\\n        {\\n            Parent = server,\\n            Charset = \\u0026#34;utf8mb4\\u0026#34;,\\n            Collation = \\u0026#34;utf8mb4_unicode_ci\\u0026#34;\\n        };\\n        infrastructure.Add(database);\\n\\n        // Add a firewall rule to allow Azure services\\n        var firewallRule = new MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule(\\u0026#34;allowAzureServices\\u0026#34;)\\n        {\\n            Parent = server,\\n            StartIPAddress = \\u0026#34;0.0.0.0\\u0026#34;,\\n            EndIPAddress = \\u0026#34;0.0.0.0\\u0026#34;\\n        };\\n        infrastructure.Add(firewallRule);\\n        #endregion\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**README location:** Insert the snippet placeholder under `## Examples` / `### Create a MySQL Flexible Server`.\\n\\n**SnippetName:** `Sample_MySqlFlexibleServer`\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nAll required sections exist in `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md` but the following are missing content:\\n\\n1. ✅ Getting started / Install the package — present\\n2. ✅ Getting started / Prerequisites — present\\n3. ❌ Getting started / Authenticate the client — **empty, needs content** (see Step 1)\\n4. ❌ Key concepts — **skeleton only, needs expansion** (see Step 1)\\n5. ❌ Examples — **empty, needs snippet placeholder** (see Steps 1 and 2)\\n6. ✅ Troubleshooting — present\\n7. ❌ Next steps — **empty, needs links** (see Step 1)\\n8. ✅ Contributing — present\\n\\nUse `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 mysql` — injects snippet code from the test file into README.md placeholders; verify README was updated with `git diff`\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 mysql` — regenerates API listing files if any public types were changed\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/` — ensures consistent formatting\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to The package adds declarative provisioning support for Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Servers, including these resource types:\\n\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServer`\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase`\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule`\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServerConfiguration`\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServerAadAdministrator`\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServerBackup` / `MySqlFlexibleServerBackupV2`\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServerMaintenance`\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServersCapability`\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServersPrivateEndpointConnection`\\n- `AdvancedThreatProtection`\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **`Authenticate the Client` section is empty** — no guidance on how to authenticate when using the provisioning library\\n- **`Examples` section is empty** — no code snippets showing how to create a MySQL Flexible Server, add a firewall rule, configure a database, etc.\\n- **`Next steps` section is empty** — no links to related docs, samples, or the Azure Developer CLI (`azd`) workflow\\n- **No sample files exist** — `tests/Samples/` directory does not exist; there are no test-backed snippets for snippet extraction\\n\\n\\u003cdetails\\u003e\\n\\u003csummary\\u003e\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\u003c/summary\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**Edit `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md`**\\n\\nReplace the empty sections with content as follows:\\n\\n1. Fill `### Authenticate the Client` with a note that no explicit authentication is needed — the provisioning library generates Bicep/ARM templates; authentication occurs when deploying via `azd` or the Azure CLI.\\n\\n2. Fill `## Examples` with at least two snippet placeholders: one for creating a basic MySQL Flexible Server and one for adding a firewall rule.\\n\\n3. Fill `## Next steps` with links to:\\n   - [Azure.Provisioning overview](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning/README.md)\\n   - Azure Developer CLI (`azd`) getting started\\n   - Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server documentation\\n\\nExample README diff for the `## Examples` section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\n### Create a MySQL Flexible Server\\n\\n```C# Snippet:MySqlProvisioningCreateFlexibleServer\\nInfrastructure infra = new();\\n\\nMySqlFlexibleServer server = new(\\\"mysqlServer\\\", MySqlFlexibleServer.ResourceVersions.V2024_12_30)\\n{\\n    Location = AzureLocation.EastUS,\\n    Sku = new MySqlFlexibleServerSku\\n    {\\n        Name = \\\"Standard_D4ds_v4\\\",\\n        Tier = ServerSkuTier.GeneralPurpose,\\n    },\\n};\\ninfra.Add(server);\\n```\\n\\n### Add a Firewall Rule\\n\\n```C# Snippet:MySqlProvisioningAddFirewallRule\\nMySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule firewallRule = new(\\\"allowAll\\\")\\n{\\n    StartIpAddress = \\\"0.0.0.0\\\",\\n    EndIpAddress = \\\"255.255.255.255\\\",\\n    Parent = server,\\n};\\ninfra.Add(firewallRule);\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n1. **Create the sample test file** at `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/tests/Samples/Sample1_MySqlProvisioning.cs`\\n\\n   Use `Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets` samples as the canonical example for test file structure. The file should:\\n   - Wrap examples in `#region Snippet:\\u003cSnippetName\\u003e` / `#endregion`\\n   - Use `[Test]` (NUnit) attribute on each method\\n   - Wrap test-only teardown/assertions in `#if !SNIPPET` / `#endif`\\n\\n   Suggested snippets:\\n   - `MySqlProvisioningCreateFlexibleServer` — demonstrates creating a `MySqlFlexibleServer`\\n   - `MySqlProvisioningAddFirewallRule` — demonstrates adding a `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule` as a child resource\\n   - `MySqlProvisioningAddDatabase` — demonstrates adding a `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase`\\n\\n   Reference an existing provisioning sample, e.g. `sdk/storage/Azure.Provisioning.Storage/tests/Samples/`, for the test class shape.\\n\\n2. **Add snippet placeholders in `README.md`** under `## Examples` in the exact format:\\n   ````\\n   ```C# Snippet:MySqlProvisioningCreateFlexibleServer\\n   ```\\n   ````\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md` must contain these sections. Currently missing/incomplete sections:\\n\\n| Section | Status |\\n|---|---|\\n| Getting started → Install the package | ✅ Present |\\n| Getting started → Prerequisites | ✅ Present |\\n| Getting started → Authenticate the Client | ❌ Empty |\\n| Key concepts | ✅ Present (brief) |\\n| Examples | ❌ Empty |\\n| Troubleshooting | ✅ Present |\\n| Next steps | ❌ Empty |\\n| Contributing | ✅ Present |\\n\\nUse `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone.\\n\\nFor the **Authenticate the Client** section, adapt the pattern from other provisioning packages (e.g. `sdk/storage/Azure.Provisioning.Storage/README.md`): note that this library generates infrastructure-as-code (Bicep) and authentication is handled at deployment time via `azd` or ARM.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/ --filter
